11089035: A cervical cancer tumor measuring approximately 41×58mm appears slightly hyperintense on T2WI, extending inferiorly to invade the anterior and posterior vaginal fornices and superiorly to the uterine isthmus, without serosal breakthrough.
11094000: A cervical cancer tumor arises from the posterior lip of the cervix, measuring approximately 46mm × 42mm, with slightly high T2 signal intensity, involving the deep muscle layer (>5mm depth), extending superiorly to the uterine isthmus and inferiorly to the external os, without serosal breakthrough.
11095169: The cervical tumor involves both the anterior and posterior lips, which appear thickened with a localized defect; no explicit T2 signal characteristics or tumor size are provided for the cervical lesion itself.
11097737: A cervical cancer tumor measuring approximately 29×18mm shows slightly high T2 signal, with expansion of the isthmus and downward invasion from the uterine body into the cervix; no clear parametrial or vaginal invasion is described.
11105988: A cervical cancer tumor involving both anterior and posterior lips measures approximately 55mm × 48mm with slightly high T2 signal intensity, invading the upper one-third of the vagina, without uterine body involvement or serosal breakthrough, and with clear fat planes preserved adjacent to the rectum and bladder.
11107079: The cervical tumor shows involvement from the uterine body lesion extending into the cervix, with the cervical stroma appearing clear and intact on T2-weighted imaging.
11108154: Cervical tumor involves the posterior wall with thickening measuring approximately 28 mm (thickness) × 37 mm (length), appearing slightly hyperintense on T2, extending superiorly to the uterine isthmus and inferiorly to the external os, without serosal breakthrough or vaginal involvement.
11109142: A cervical cancer tumor is located at the posterior lip of the cervix, measuring approximately 14×16mm, with irregular slightly high T2 signal, unclear boundaries, no serosal breakthrough, and no vaginal involvement.
11111459: A cervical cancer tumor measuring approximately 28×22mm is identified in the cervix, showing slightly high T2 signal intensity with relatively clear margins, involving the posterior vaginal fornix but without obvious paracervical or lower vaginal invasion.
11111719: A cervical tumor measuring approximately 27×34mm appears slightly hyperintense on T2-weighted images, extending from the cervix to the uterine isthmus level with invasion of the lower one-third of the vagina, without serosal breakthrough.
11115283: A ring-shaped cervical tumor measures 34×50mm, appearing slightly hyperintense on T2WI, involving the mucosal, stromal, and muscular layers with intact serosa, extending superiorly to the uterine body and inferiorly to the anterior and posterior vaginal fornices.
11118117: A cervical cancer tumor measures approximately 33×30mm, appearing slightly hyperintense on T2-weighted imaging, extending superiorly to the uterine isthmus level, with vaginal filling material noted but no definite invasion, and no serosal breakthrough.
11120871: A cervical cancer tumor involves both the anterior and posterior lips (predominantly posterior), measuring approximately 53mm × 51mm, showing slightly high T2 signal, with the posterior lip extending to the subserosa and the upper margin reaching the uterine isthmus; vaginal walls are uninvolved.
11123180: A cervical cancer tumor involving both anterior and posterior lips measures approximately 51mm × 40mm, showing slightly high T2 signal intensity, confined to the cervix with intact serosal layer and clear fat planes separating it from adjacent structures.
11131144: A cervical cancer tumor measuring approximately 50mm × 43mm shows low T2 signal with clear boundaries, involving the anterior vaginal wall, with irregular uterine serosal surface and indistinct margin with the upper rectum.
11134429: A cervical cancer tumor centered on the anterior lip measures approximately 46×33mm, appearing slightly hyperintense on T2-weighted images, with invasion into the upper one-third of the vagina inferiorly and no serosal breakthrough superiorly.
